purpose: AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace covering a completed self-target command buffer that decodes to write-buf-one and appends literal 1.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel on the triangular self command-buffer write-buffer node.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel on the triangular self command-buffer write-buffer node.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel on the triangular self command-buffer write-buffer node.

layers.summary: A matching one-hot standard input completes buffer 00111, decodes it as self/write-buf-one, and appends literal 1 as the new buffer.
layers.boundary: This layer states only the self-target write-buffer command-buffer slice; init-family dispatch, standard-signal commands, and recipient non-init command messages remain separate boundaries.

layers.summary: The self command-buffer write-buffer node uses the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the other traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete command-routing circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one completed self-target write-buffer command.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the ADR-0161 command-buffer write-buffer behavior and adds no recipient command-message execution.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary command-routing support remain candidate interpretations.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested command-buffer write-buffer dispatch.

routed_signal_flow: command buffer consumed; buffer reset to literal append
